# CI: require ≥1 `Resolves #N` in agent PR bodies (forbid Closes/Fixes; Refs/Related allowed as extra)

## Goal

Enforce the **1-PR-per-ticket model** at the CI gate. Operator-directed (2026-06-02): every agent/`ai`-labeled PR body MUST contain at least one `Resolves #N` — the *only* sanctioned closing keyword (= delivered work). This mechanically prevents ticket-fragmentation: a ticket that needs N PRs can't have N valid `Resolves` (only one PR can resolve it), so it's forced to be an **epic + subs** or split into multiple tickets. Empirical trigger: ~27 PRs were opened for the single ticket #11925 (all `Refs #11925`, none resolving it) — the current check accepts `Refs`/`Related`, so it didn't catch the fragmentation.

## The rule (exact, operator-specified)

- **MUST contain ≥1 `Resolves #N`** — the only accepted closing keyword (delivered work).
- **`Closes #N` is forbidden** — `Closes` means closed-without-delivery (not-planned / superseded / dropped); that needs *no PR*, so a PR must never use it.
- **`Fixes #N` is forbidden** — ambiguous; use `Resolves`.
- **`Refs #N` / `Related: #N` are allowed** as *additional* references, and multiple `Resolves` lines are fine — but they do not satisfy the requirement; ≥1 `Resolves` is mandatory.

## Scope

- `.github/workflows/agent-pr-body-lint.yml` — replace the permissive ticket regex `(Resolves|Closes|Fixes|Related:|Refs)\s+#\d+` with: require ≥1 `Resolves\s+#\d+`; fail if `Closes`/`Fixes #N` appears; update the violation message.
- `.agents/skills/pull-request/references/pull-request-workflow.md` §3.3 — sync the close-keyword policy to "Resolves-only; ≥1 mandatory; Closes/Fixes forbidden; Refs/Related allowed as additional" and reconcile the partial-resolution guidance (a contributing PR `Resolves` its own sub-ticket and `Refs` the parent — it never resolves *nothing*).

## Contract Ledger

| Surface | Input | Rule | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| `agent-pr-body-lint` workflow | agent/`ai` PR body | ≥1 `Resolves #N`; no `Closes`/`Fixes #N`; `Refs`/`Related` + extra `Resolves` allowed | pass, or fail + guidance comment |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. PR body with ≥1 `Resolves #N` (plus optional extra `Refs`/`Resolves`) passes.
2. PR body with only `Refs`/`Related` (no `Resolves`) fails.
3. PR body containing `Closes #N` or `Fixes #N` fails with the explanation (Closes = no-delivery/no-PR; Fixes = ambiguous → use Resolves).
4. Non-agent, non-`ai` PRs remain skipped (unchanged).
5. `pull-request-workflow.md` §3.3 wording matches the enforced rule.

Related: #11501 (original agent PR-body lint).

## Timeline

- 2026-06-02T08:04:17Z @neo-opus-4-7 added the `enhancement` label
- 2026-06-02T08:04:18Z @neo-opus-4-7 added the `ai` label
- 2026-06-02T08:14:30Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #12368
- 2026-06-02T08:21:42Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `6027de4` - "feat(ci): require a Resolves #N in agent PR bodies; forbid Closes/Fixes (#12367)

agent-pr-body-lint.yml previously accepted any of Resolves|Closes|Fixes|Related:|Refs
#N as the ticket reference. That let a single ticket be fragmented across many PRs
(each `Refs #N`, none resolving it) — the gap that produced ~27 PRs for the one
ticket #11925.

Strengthen the check so every agent/`ai` PR body MUST contain at least one
`Resolves #N` — the only sanctioned closing keyword (= delivered work). This
mechanically enforces the 1-PR-per-ticket model: a ticket that needs N PRs cannot
have N valid `Resolves`, so it must become an epic + subs or be split.

- `Closes #N` is now forbidden: Closes = closed-without-delivery (not-planned /
  superseded / dropped) -> that outcome needs no PR.
- `Fixes #N` is now forbidden: ambiguous; use `Resolves`.
- `Refs #N` / `Related: #N` remain allowed as additional references, and multiple
  `Resolves` lines are fine — but they no longer satisfy the requirement alone.

Also syncs pull-request-workflow.md §3.3 (close-keyword policy) + the Epic
Close-Target Ban + the branch-history hygiene note to match the enforced rule.

* docs(pr-review): sync Resolves-only close-target audit (#12367)

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-06-02T16:01:54Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-06-04T02:53:19Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by #12474
- 2026-06-04T03:10:06Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `a37942c` - "feat(agentos): bake leaf-sub close-target contract into epic-create (#12454)

Incorporate @neo-claude-opus's friction→gold input: each sub MUST be a one-PR-deliverable leaf that cleanly Resolves (the #12367 lint-pr-body contract); the epic is the Refs'd umbrella, never a PR close-target (epic-auto-close-with-open-subs hazard); multi-cause symptoms = one leaf per cause. Prevents the mid-review leaf-splitting churn seen on #12470/#12472.

Refs #12454"
- 2026-06-04T03:10:08Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #12476
- 2026-06-04T08:23:27Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5e0cea1` - "feat(agentos): add epic-create skill — epic bodies = problem-scope + intended-solution (#12476)

* feat(agentos): add epic-create skill — epic bodies = problem-scope + intended-solution (#12454)

New .agents/skills/epic-create/ (creation-side dual of ticket-create; entry partner of epic-review/epic-resolution). Codifies: epic body = problem-scope + intended-solution; ACs live in SUB tickets; subs LINKED via update_issue_relationship + added incrementally; body MUST NOT hardcode a sub-list (stales out → FAIL). SKILL router + references payload + manifest entry + claude symlink + skill-catalog rows.

Resolves #12454

* feat(agentos): bake leaf-sub close-target contract into epic-create (#12454)

Incorporate @neo-claude-opus's friction→gold input: each sub MUST be a one-PR-deliverable leaf that cleanly Resolves (the #12367 lint-pr-body contract); the epic is the Refs'd umbrella, never a PR close-target (epic-auto-close-with-open-subs hazard); multi-cause symptoms = one leaf per cause. Prevents the mid-review leaf-splitting churn seen on #12470/#12472.

Refs #12454

* docs(skills): epic carve-out in ticket-create AC mandate — compose with epic-create (#12454)

Resolve the cross-skill contradiction @neo-gpt flagged on PR #12476: ticket-create §5 mandated ACs for every ticket body, but epic-create forbids ACs in epic bodies. Add a narrow epic carve-out at the AC bullet pointing to epic-create (epic body = problem-scope + intended-solution; ACs in subs; subs linked + leaf-sized). The two creation-side skills now compose.

Refs #12454
